narrative: Your law school application was denied because our predictive model, which assesses the likelihood of passing the bar exam on the first try based on academic and background factors, estimated a 42% chance that you would not pass—above the threshold we use for admissions decisions. This prediction stemmed primarily from your Year 3 school decile ranking, which placed you in the bottom tenth of your peers, strongly signaling to the model a higher risk of bar exam struggles compared to students who typically pass, who average around the sixth decile. That factor had the single biggest pull toward the failure prediction, outweighing others in influence. On a more positive note, your LSAT score of 39 stood out as a major strength, well above the average of 37 for those who pass, and it significantly pushed the model's assessment toward expecting success. Similarly, your undergraduate GPA of 3.00, while slightly below the 3.24 average for passers, still contributed helpfully by nudging the prediction in a favorable direction. Your lower-middle family income bracket aligns with a smaller but present group among bar passers (about 9%), and your choice of full-time study matches the overwhelming majority (over 93%) of successful students, though these played lesser roles here. Overall, the model saw your Year 3 performance as the dominant concern, not fully offset by your stronger LSAT and solid undergrad record, setting you apart from typical admitted students whose consistent decile rankings are much higher. This isn't a judgment on your potential or background—it's a data-driven forecast to help the school select those with the highest predicted bar success rates. To move forward, consider retaking courses or programs to boost your later-year deciles, as that's the key lever here, and perhaps seek targeted LSAT or bar prep resources to build on your already impressive score. You might also explore post-baccalaureate options or community college pathways that have helped others in similar income situations strengthen their profiles. Reapplying after addressing these could shift the odds significantly in your favor. Keep pushing—you've got strengths that shine through in this analysis.
